Brainstorm_DecisionMaker_2_2_0_Info.keywords: DecisionSupport; WeightedChoice; LangGraph; OpenRouter; CoVe; AntiHallucination; AntiLoop; VFS; VerticalFeatureSliced; LDD; LogDrivenDevelopment; SemanticExoskeleton; AsyncIO; AsyncSqliteSaver; Tavily; AsyncTavilyClient; asyncioGather; HumanInTheLoop; PluggableAdapter; DependencyInjection; PytestAsyncio; ReasoningModelNormalization; ThinkingTagStripping; OutputWrapperExtraction; CentralisedSanitization; IdempotentPipeline; GrokCompat; DeepSeekR1Compat; O1Compat; JsonRawDecode; SmokeRun; BlockSanitize; HybridReasoningRouting; PerNodeReasoningPolicy; MechanicalVsAnalyticalNodes; LatencyOptimization; ReasoningEnabledFlag
Brainstorm_DecisionMaker_2_2_0_Info.terms: Dilemma; Weights; ToolFacts; CritiqueFeedback; RewriteCount; CheckpointerThreadId; InterruptAfter; JsonFenceParsing; DoubleTrueError; BeliefState; BlockAntiLoop; BlockDoubleTrueGuard; BlockExecuteSearches; BlockSanitize; PerCallCheckpointerContext; FakeLlmFactory; FakeSearchAsync; MemorySaver; ThinkingBlock; ReasoningBlock; ReflectionBlock; OutputBlock; OutputWrapper; LastBlockPreference; RawDecodeFallback; Strategy0Normalization; SanitizePipeline; CleanedLength; SanitizedDelta; GrokFast41; BugFixContext; Idempotent; NodeReasoningPolicy; MechanicalNode; AnalyticalNode; ReasoningEnabledOverride; OpenRouterExtraBody; ReasoningOffBodyFlag
Brainstorm_DecisionMaker_2_2_0_Info.annotation: Backend implementation of Scenario 1 ("Complex Choice / Decision Maker") for the Gnome-Consultant multi-agent system. Infrastructure v2.2.0 — fully asynchronous orchestration core + centralised LLM response sanitization + Hybrid Reasoning Routing (T3: per-node reasoning policy — mechanical routing/parsing/NL-question nodes disable native chain-of-thought for latency/cost; analytical draft/critique/synthesis nodes keep reasoning ON for arithmetic-audit quality). A LangGraph-orchestrated analytical loop with 7 nodes, a Chain-of-Verification (CoVe) self-critique loop capped by an Anti-Loop guard, pluggable Tavily-backed async search with parallel query execution, and a human-in-the-loop interrupt that requests weight calibration from the user. Isolated under Vertical Feature Sliced (VFS) architecture: `src/core/` hosts reusable utilities (llm_client accepts `reasoning_enabled: Optional[bool]` — domain-agnostic); `src/features/decision_maker/` owns feature-specific `_NODE_REASONING_POLICY` map — concerns cleanly separated. LLM calls are routed through OpenRouter via ChatOpenAI.ainvoke() with optional `extra_body={"reasoning":{"enabled":false}}` per-node body parameter. Checkpointing uses AsyncSqliteSaver (production) or MemorySaver (tests) via Dependency Injection. Observability is guaranteed by strict LDD log formatting (`[CLASSIFIER][IMP:N][FN][BLOCK][OP] msg [STATUS]`) emitted into a single local file `decision_maker.log`; per-query IMP:7/IMP:8 pairing is preserved across concurrent branches.

BusinessScenarios.Scenario: AsyncCaller -> await start_session(user_input, thread_id) -> AsyncSqliteSaver context opens -> graph runs until interrupt_after=3_Weight_Questioner -> returns {"status":"awaiting_user","question":...,"thread_id":...}

AsyncCaller -> await resume_session(user_answer, thread_id) -> AsyncSqliteSaver reopens SAME DB -> aupdate_state injects user_answer -> graph runs Node3.5 -> Node4 -> Node5 (up to 1 rewrite) -> Node6 -> returns {"status":"done","final_answer":...,"thread_id":...}
tool_node -> builds coros=[search_fn(q) for q in state.search_queries] -> await asyncio.gather(*coros) -> per-query IMP:7/IMP:8 pairs logged (interleaving allowed) -> flattens results into tool_facts
Internal -> cove_critique audits draft against facts -> on rewrite_count>=2 force-approves to break potential loop (Anti-Loop safety IMP:10 BLOCK_ANTI_LOOP)
Internal -> context_analyzer emits routing flags -> route_from_context (sync) raises DoubleTrueError if needs_data AND ready_for_weights are both True

search_async -> reads TAVILY_API_KEY at call-time -> if set: _build_tavily_adapter() -> AsyncTavilyClient.search() -> normalized result list; else: stub_search_async() -> deterministic single-item list

safe_json_parse -> _strip_reasoning_blocks (thin wrapper over llm_utils.strip_reasoning_blocks as of v1.2.0) removes <thinking>/<think>/<reasoning>/<reflection> prefix -> Strategy 1..4 run on clean text -> if Strategy 4 greedy regex fails, Pass B json.JSONDecoder().raw_decode() finds first balanced JSON -> returns dict; JsonParseError.raw_snippet preserves ORIGINAL input

Any node -> await _invoke_llm_async(llm, system, human, node_name) -> ChatOpenAI.ainvoke -> raw_response = response.content -> sanitize_llm_response(raw) [strip_reasoning_blocks -> extract_output_payload (prefers LAST <output> block) -> .strip()] -> cleaned -> [API][IMP:8][BLOCK_SANITIZE] raw_length + cleaned_length + sanitized_delta logged -> return cleaned to caller node. All 6 LLM nodes (context_analyzer, weight_questioner, weight_parser, draft_generator, cove_critique, final_synthesizer) receive pre-sanitized payload; final_answer is therefore guaranteed free of <thinking>/<output>/<reasoning>/<reflection> tag leaks.

Caller node body -> looks up `_NODE_REASONING_POLICY["<node_name>"]` -> calls `build_llm(reasoning_enabled=<bool>)` -> llm_client resolves flag (explicit arg > env > default True) -> when False, injects `extra_body={"reasoning":{"enabled":false}}` into ChatOpenAI -> OpenRouter forwards body param to provider (xAI/DeepSeek/OpenAI) which skips native chain-of-thought emission. Mechanical nodes (context_analyzer, weight_questioner, weight_parser) run reasoning=OFF — fast JSON/short NL. Analytical nodes (draft_generator, cove_critique, final_synthesizer) run reasoning=ON — preserve multi-step arithmetic/audit quality. Measured impact (grok-4.1-fast, smoke_fb6488ad): start_session -45% wall-clock, total -5%, quality preserved, cost flat.

src_core_llm_client_py.keywords: OpenRouter; ChatOpenAI; LLMFactory; EnvDriven; BaseURL; Ainvoke; ReasoningEnabledFlag; HybridReasoningRouting; PerCallOverride; ExtraBody; DomainAgnostic; BackwardsCompatEnv
src_core_llm_client_py.annotation: UPDATED in v2.2.0 (T3 — Hybrid Reasoning Routing). Factory for the OpenRouter-backed ChatOpenAI client. Reads OPENROUTER_MODEL and OPENROUTER_API_KEY from environment (no defaults — fails loudly). Adds OpenRouter attribution headers. v1.1.0 introduced the OPENROUTER_REASONING_ENABLED env toggle (falsy values "0"/"false"/"no"/"off" disable native chain-of-thought via `extra_body={"reasoning":{"enabled":false}}`). v1.2.0 adds an EXPLICIT `reasoning_enabled: Optional[bool]` parameter that overrides env when not None — enabling Hybrid Reasoning Routing (T3) where each feature module (e.g. decision_maker) decides per-node whether reasoning is on or off. The factory itself stays DOMAIN-AGNOSTIC: per-node policy belongs to the caller (decision_maker's `_NODE_REASONING_POLICY` map), keeping llm_client reusable across features. ChatOpenAI in langchain-openai 0.2.x natively supports await llm.ainvoke(messages) — no code change was required for async migration.

build_llm_FUNC.NAME: build_llm
build_llm_FUNC.keywords: LLMFactory; OpenRouter; ChatOpenAI; Ainvoke; ReasoningEnabledParameter; ExtraBodyInjection; EnvFallback
build_llm_FUNC.annotation: Signature in v1.2.0: `def build_llm(temperature: float = 0.2, reasoning_enabled: Optional[bool] = None) -> ChatOpenAI`. Resolves reasoning flag with precedence explicit-arg > env OPENROUTER_REASONING_ENABLED > default True. When resolved to False, injects `extra_body={"reasoning":{"enabled":false}}` into ChatOpenAI kwargs — OpenRouter forwards the body parameter to the provider (xAI grok, DeepSeek, OpenAI o-series) which skips native `<thinking>`/`<reasoning>` block emission. Clients that don't support the toggle silently ignore it. Client supports both llm.invoke() (sync) and llm.ainvoke() (async) natively.

src_core_logger_py.keywords: LDD; Logger; DualHandler; FileHandler; StreamHandler; Idempotent; AsyncSafe
src_core_logger_py.annotation: UNCHANGED in v2.0.0. Idempotent LDD logger factory. Attaches a FileHandler writing to brainstorm/decision_maker.log and a StreamHandler writing to stdout. Python's stdlib logging is thread- and event-loop-safe; async callers emit via the same named logger without contention.

setup_ldd_logger_FUNC.NAME: setup_ldd_logger
setup_ldd_logger_FUNC.annotation: Returns the named logger "decision_maker"; guarded against double-handler registration via hasHandlers() check.

src_core_llm_utils_py.keywords: LLMOutputSanitization; ReasoningModelNormalization; OutputWrapperExtraction; Idempotent; Pipeline; StdlibOnly; CentralizedSanitizer; BlockSanitize; BUG_FIX_CONTEXT; GrokFast41; DeepSeekR1; O1Family
src_core_llm_utils_py.annotation: NEW in v2.1.0 (Option D — centralised LLM response sanitization). Canonical stdlib-only (re) sanitization utilities applied ONCE at the single _invoke_llm_async call site so all six LLM nodes receive a pre-cleaned payload. Three pure idempotent functions form a small pipeline. Fixes two concrete production leaks observed on grok-4.1-fast: (1) weight_questioner silently returning last_question=''; (2) final_synthesizer leaking raw <thinking>/<output> tags into final_answer. Also preserves diagnostic trace: _invoke_llm_async logs BOTH raw_length (before sanitize) AND cleaned_length+sanitized_delta (after sanitize) — full visibility into how much was stripped per node per call. Pipeline is intentionally idempotent so repeated invocations are safe; extract_output_payload on absent/malformed <output> returns input unchanged (no data loss on non-reasoning models).

strip_reasoning_blocks_FUNC.NAME: strip_reasoning_blocks
strip_reasoning_blocks_FUNC.keywords: ReasoningTagRegex; DOTALL; IGNORECASE; ThinkingBlock; ReflectionBlock; Idempotent; StdlibRe
strip_reasoning_blocks_FUNC.annotation: Regex-strips <thinking>, <think>, <reasoning>, <reflection> block-pairs using `re.sub(r'<(thinking|think|reasoning|reflection)\b[^>]*>.*?</\1\s*>', '', raw, flags=re.DOTALL | re.IGNORECASE)`. Handles nested example braces inside reasoning prose (they are removed with the block). Idempotent — applying twice yields the same result. Generalises across Grok 4.1 (<thinking>), DeepSeek-R1 (<think>), o1-family (<reasoning>), and <reflection> for future-compat.

extract_output_payload_FUNC.NAME: extract_output_payload
extract_output_payload_FUNC.annotation: If <output>...</output> wrapper present, returns the inner content trimmed. If wrapper absent/malformed, returns input UNCHANGED (critical — no data loss for non-reasoning models). When MULTIPLE <output> blocks are present, returns the LAST complete one — reasoning models occasionally echo the prompt's JSON example in their reasoning before writing their own structured answer; the last block is the authoritative output. Case-insensitive, DOTALL.

sanitize_llm_response_FUNC.NAME: sanitize_llm_response
sanitize_llm_response_FUNC.annotation: Public pipeline entry point. Chains: strip_reasoning_blocks(raw) -> extract_output_payload(...) -> .strip(). Idempotent: sanitize(sanitize(x)) == sanitize(x) for all inputs (including empty string, whitespace-only, and already-clean payloads). ALWAYS returns a str. Called exactly once from _invoke_llm_async in nodes.py v2.1.0; all 6 LLM nodes receive the post-pipeline result.

src_core_json_utils_py.keywords: JsonFence; SafeParse; JsonParseError; RawSnippet; PureCPU; DefenceInDepth; ThinWrapper; ReasoningModelNormalization; RawDecodeFallback; Strategy0; BugFixContext
src_core_json_utils_py.annotation: UPDATED in v1.2.0 — _strip_reasoning_blocks collapsed to a thin wrapper delegating to llm_utils.strip_reasoning_blocks (DRY — canonical regex now lives in one place). Five-stage extraction pipeline preserved: Strategy 0 (normalisation via wrapper), Strategy 1 (raw json.loads), Strategy 2 (```json fenced), Strategy 3 (bare ``` fenced), Strategy 4 (greedy balanced-brace regex + Pass B json.JSONDecoder().raw_decode() fallback). Strategy 0 retained as DEFENCE-IN-DEPTH — harmless no-op on already-clean input produced by _invoke_llm_async, but protects any caller that bypasses that code path (e.g. future offline batch processors). Raises JsonParseError with raw_snippet preserving the ORIGINAL pre-normalisation input. No I/O — safe to call from async context.

JsonParseError_CLASS.NAME: JsonParseError
JsonParseError_CLASS.annotation: UNCHANGED in v1.2.0. Typed exception carrying raw_snippet (the ORIGINAL pre-parse text, NOT post-normalisation — invariant for LDD diagnostics) for IMP:10 ExceptionEnrichment logs.

safe_json_parse_FUNC.NAME: safe_json_parse
safe_json_parse_FUNC.annotation: Public parser. Unchanged flow in v1.2.0: (1) captures original input for raw_snippet; (2) invokes _strip_reasoning_blocks (thin wrapper over llm_utils.strip_reasoning_blocks); (3) runs Strategies 1..4 on `normalized`; (4) Strategy 4 Pass B invokes json.JSONDecoder().raw_decode(normalized, first_brace_idx) on regex failure; (5) on total failure raises JsonParseError(raw_snippet=original[:200]). Returns dict on success.

_strip_reasoning_blocks_FUNC.NAME: _strip_reasoning_blocks
_strip_reasoning_blocks_FUNC.annotation: MODIFIED in v1.2.0 — now a THIN WRAPPER delegating to llm_utils.strip_reasoning_blocks. Keeps the internal symbol and Strategy 0 call chain in safe_json_parse unchanged for minimal diff. Canonical regex logic lives in llm_utils; this wrapper exists purely for backwards-compat and defence-in-depth for callers that bypass _invoke_llm_async.

src_features_decision_maker_state_py.annotation: UNCHANGED in v2.0.0 (AC4). DecisionMakerState TypedDict with exactly 13 fields: user_input, is_data_sufficient, needs_data, ready_for_weights, search_queries, tool_facts, last_question, user_answer, weights, assumptions, draft_analysis, critique_feedback, rewrite_count, final_answer. Schema frozen to prevent graph-topology drift during infrastructure refactoring.

DecisionMakerState_CLASS.NAME: DecisionMakerState
DecisionMakerState_CLASS.annotation: Typed state bag carried by LangGraph across all 7 nodes. Fields are progressively populated by node functions; the graph topology relies on specific flag combinations for routing.

src_features_decision_maker_prompts_py.annotation: UNCHANGED in v2.0.0 (hard invariant — business-logic freeze). Verbatim prompt constants: GLOBAL_PRIMING, NODE_1_CONTEXT_ANALYZER_PROMPT, NODE_3_WEIGHT_QUESTIONER_PROMPT, NODE_35_WEIGHT_PARSER_PROMPT, NODE_4_DRAFT_GENERATOR_PROMPT, NODE_5_COVE_CRITIQUE_PROMPT, NODE_6_FINAL_SYNTHESIZER_PROMPT. No LLM calls — constants only.

src_features_decision_maker_tools_py.keywords: PluggableAdapter; AsyncIO; Tavily; AsyncTavilyClient; StubSearch; PerCallResolution; LDDPerQuery
src_features_decision_maker_tools_py.annotation: REWRITTEN in v2.0.0. Pluggable async search abstraction. Public search_async(query) resolves the adapter at call-time: Tavily when TAVILY_API_KEY is set, else stub_search_async. Private _build_tavily_adapter() constructs AsyncTavilyClient(api_key=...) and returns a normalising async callable. Emits [API][IMP:7]...query={q!r}...[PENDING] before the outbound call and [API][IMP:8]...query={q!r} items={n}...[SUCCESS] on response — one pair per query. Return shape: List[Dict[query,result,source]] matching tool_facts list semantics.

search_async_FUNC.NAME: search_async
search_async_FUNC.annotation: Public async entry. Resolves adapter at call-time via TAVILY_API_KEY presence check. Emits the IMP:7 PENDING marker, awaits the adapter, emits IMP:8 SUCCESS marker with item count. Returns List[Dict] suitable for flattening into tool_facts.

stub_search_async_FUNC.NAME: stub_search_async
stub_search_async_FUNC.annotation: Deterministic async stub: `await asyncio.sleep(0)` + returns [{"query": q, "result": "<stubbed-fact>", "source": "stub"}]. Selected automatically when TAVILY_API_KEY is absent — guarantees tests and offline development never make real network calls.

_build_tavily_adapter_FUNC.NAME: _build_tavily_adapter
_build_tavily_adapter_FUNC.annotation: Private factory. Constructs AsyncTavilyClient(api_key=...) and returns a bound async callable `async def adapter(query) -> List[Dict]` that normalises Tavily's .search() response to the {query, result, source} shape. Encapsulates backend-specific construction — allows future swap of search provider without touching search_async.

src_features_decision_maker_nodes_py.keywords: LangGraphNodes; AsyncDef; AsyncioGather; AntiLoop; DoubleTrueError; CoVe; LDDMarkers; DependencyInjection; Routers; PureStateInspector; LLMOutputSanitization; BlockSanitize; CentralisedCleaning; HybridReasoningRouting; NodeReasoningPolicy; MechanicalNodes; AnalyticalNodes; ReasoningEnabledPerNode
src_features_decision_maker_nodes_py.annotation: UPDATED to v2.2.0 (T3 — Hybrid Reasoning Routing). v2.1.0 sanitization pipeline preserved verbatim. ONLY CHANGE: NEW module-level constant `_NODE_REASONING_POLICY: dict[str, bool]` classifies each of 6 LLM nodes as MECHANICAL (reasoning=False: context_analyzer, weight_questioner, weight_parser) or ANALYTICAL (reasoning=True: draft_generator, cove_critique, final_synthesizer); all 6 `build_llm()` call-sites updated to `build_llm(reasoning_enabled=_NODE_REASONING_POLICY["<node_name>"])`. `_invoke_llm_async`, sanitization pipeline, LDD markers, node bodies, `tool_node`, and routers UNCHANGED. Safety invariants preserved verbatim: DoubleTrueError in route_from_context on mutually exclusive flags; Anti-Loop cap in cove_critique forces needs_rewrite=False when rewrite_count>=2 and emits [LOGIC][IMP:10][BLOCK_ANTI_LOOP][SafetyTripped]. Real-network A/B (grok-4.1-fast, smoke_fb6488ad): start_session -45% wall-clock (mechanical nodes), total -5%, LLM calls 9 (parity), cost flat, CoVe arithmetic audit quality preserved.

_NODE_REASONING_POLICY_CONST.NAME: _NODE_REASONING_POLICY
_NODE_REASONING_POLICY_CONST.keywords: ModuleConstant; HybridReasoningRouting; NodeClassification; MechanicalVsAnalytical; PolicyMap; DomainPolicy
_NODE_REASONING_POLICY_CONST.annotation: NEW in v2.2.0. Module-level `dict[str, bool]` mapping LLM node names to their reasoning policy. Keys mirror the 6 LLM node function names; values are True (reasoning ON — analytical) or False (reasoning OFF — mechanical). Classification rationale lives in the inline RATIONALE block above the dict. Read at each LLM call-site to parameterise `build_llm(reasoning_enabled=...)`. The boundary between mechanical and analytical is task-specific (decision_maker concern) and therefore lives in the feature module rather than the domain-agnostic llm_client factory. Concrete mapping: context_analyzer=False (JSON routing flags), weight_questioner=False (single short NL question), weight_parser=False (JSON weights extraction), draft_generator=True (math-heavy weighted comparison), cove_critique=True (arithmetic audit — caught FV-annuity bug end-to-end), final_synthesizer=True (multi-section structured Markdown synthesis).

DoubleTrueError_CLASS.NAME: DoubleTrueError
DoubleTrueError_CLASS.annotation: UNCHANGED class. Raised only inside sync router route_from_context when context_analyzer emits both needs_data=True and ready_for_weights=True. Indicates LLM prompt-following failure; treated as fatal routing error.

_invoke_llm_async_FUNC.NAME: _invoke_llm_async
_invoke_llm_async_FUNC.keywords: PrivateAsyncHelper; Ainvoke; LLMCall; LDDPair; LLMOutputSanitization; BlockSanitize; CleanedLengthLog; CentralisedCleaning
_invoke_llm_async_FUNC.annotation: UPDATED in v2.1.0 (Option D). Signature: `async def _invoke_llm_async(llm, system_text, human_text, node_name) -> str`. Flow: builds [SystemMessage, HumanMessage], emits [API][IMP:7][BLOCK_LLM_CALL][ExternalCall] PENDING, awaits llm.ainvoke(messages), captures raw_response = response.content, emits [API][IMP:8][BLOCK_LLM_CALL][ResponseReceived] raw_length=N SUCCESS, calls `cleaned = sanitize_llm_response(raw_response)` from llm_utils, emits NEW [API][IMP:8][BLOCK_SANITIZE][Sanitized] cleaned_length=M sanitized_delta=(N-M) SUCCESS, returns `cleaned` (NOT raw_response). All callers (6 LLM nodes) now receive sanitized payload without any change to their own bodies.

context_analyzer_FUNC.NAME: context_analyzer
context_analyzer_FUNC.keywords: Node1; AsyncDef; JSONParse; RoutingFlags; IMP5; IMP9; MechanicalNode; ReasoningOff
context_analyzer_FUNC.annotation: Node 1 — async. `async def context_analyzer(state, llm_factory=None) -> dict`. MECHANICAL node (T3): calls `build_llm(reasoning_enabled=_NODE_REASONING_POLICY["context_analyzer"])` which is False — native chain-of-thought disabled for this JSON-emitting routing step. Awaits _invoke_llm_async(GLOBAL_PRIMING+NODE_1_prompt, human_text). Parses JSON via safe_json_parse, writes needs_data / ready_for_weights / is_data_sufficient / search_queries flags to state. Post-parse state write identical to v1.

tool_node_FUNC.NAME: tool_node
tool_node_FUNC.keywords: Node2; AsyncDef; AsyncioGather; ParallelSearch; SearchFnDI; PerQueryLDD
tool_node_FUNC.annotation: Node 2 — async + parallel. `async def tool_node(state, search_fn=None) -> dict`. Resolves search_fn (default: tools.search_async). Builds coros=[search_fn(q) for q in state.search_queries]; awaits results=await asyncio.gather(*coros). Flattens via itertools.chain.from_iterable and appends to tool_facts. Parallel-execution invariant: N queries complete in ~max(single) wall-time rather than sum. Per-query IMP:7/IMP:8 LDD pairs emitted (interleaving permitted per AC13).

weight_questioner_FUNC.NAME: weight_questioner
weight_questioner_FUNC.keywords: Node3; AsyncDef; CalibrationQuestion; InterruptTarget; MechanicalNode; ReasoningOff
weight_questioner_FUNC.annotation: Node 3 — async. MECHANICAL node (T3): `build_llm(reasoning_enabled=_NODE_REASONING_POLICY["weight_questioner"])` = False — single short NL question, no multi-step reasoning needed. Generates calibration question and writes last_question. Graph interrupts AFTER this node via interrupt_after=["3_Weight_Questioner"]. Identical body to v1 except async def + await _invoke_llm_async.

weight_parser_FUNC.NAME: weight_parser
weight_parser_FUNC.keywords: Node3_5; AsyncDef; ParseUserAnswer; WeightsJSON; MechanicalNode; ReasoningOff
weight_parser_FUNC.annotation: Node 3.5 — async. MECHANICAL node (T3): `build_llm(reasoning_enabled=_NODE_REASONING_POLICY["weight_parser"])` = False — pure JSON extraction from user's NL answer. Parses human user_answer into weights dict (and optional assumptions). Identical body to v1 except async def + await _invoke_llm_async.

draft_generator_FUNC.NAME: draft_generator
draft_generator_FUNC.keywords: Node4; AsyncDef; DraftAnalysis; MarkdownDraft; AnalyticalNode; ReasoningOn
draft_generator_FUNC.annotation: Node 4 — async. ANALYTICAL node (T3): `build_llm(reasoning_enabled=_NODE_REASONING_POLICY["draft_generator"])` = True — math-heavy weighted comparison over tool_facts requires native chain-of-thought. Generates draft_analysis from dilemma + weights + tool_facts (+ optional critique_feedback on rewrite). Identical body to v1 except async def + await _invoke_llm_async.

cove_critique_FUNC.NAME: cove_critique
cove_critique_FUNC.keywords: Node5; AsyncDef; CoVe; AntiLoop; SafetyCap; BlockAntiLoop; IMP10; AnalyticalNode; ReasoningOn; ArithmeticAudit
cove_critique_FUNC.annotation: Node 5 — async CoVe auditor. ANALYTICAL node (T3): `build_llm(reasoning_enabled=_NODE_REASONING_POLICY["cove_critique"])` = True — arithmetic audit with native reasoning (empirically caught FV-annuity bug on real grok-4.1-fast traffic). Anti-Loop cap PRESERVED VERBATIM: if rewrite_count>=2, force needs_rewrite=False and emit [LOGIC][IMP:10][cove_critique][BLOCK_ANTI_LOOP][SafetyTripped] log line. Otherwise, if LLM requests rewrite, increment rewrite_count.

final_synthesizer_FUNC.NAME: final_synthesizer
final_synthesizer_FUNC.keywords: Node6; AsyncDef; FinalAnswer; Markdown; TerminalNode; AnalyticalNode; ReasoningOn
final_synthesizer_FUNC.annotation: Node 6 — async. ANALYTICAL node (T3): `build_llm(reasoning_enabled=_NODE_REASONING_POLICY["final_synthesizer"])` = True — multi-section structured Markdown synthesis benefits from native reasoning for consistency. Packages final_answer Markdown from accumulated state. Identical body to v1 except async def + await _invoke_llm_async.

route_from_context_FUNC.NAME: route_from_context
route_from_context_FUNC.annotation: SYNC. Pure state inspector, no I/O. Raises DoubleTrueError when both needs_data and ready_for_weights are True. Returns "tool" when needs_data-only, "questioner" when ready_for_weights-only. LangGraph accepts mixed sync/async across nodes and routers.

route_from_critique_FUNC.NAME: route_from_critique
route_from_critique_FUNC.annotation: SYNC. Pure state inspector. Returns "rewrite" iff critique_feedback non-empty AND rewrite_count<2; else "finalize". Signature and behaviour unchanged from v1.

src_features_decision_maker_graph_py.keywords: StateGraphAssembly; AsyncSessionAPI; AsyncSqliteSaver; PerCallCheckpointerContext; DependencyInjection; InterruptAfter; ConceptA
src_features_decision_maker_graph_py.annotation: REFACTORED in v2.0.0. Graph assembly factored into a SYNC `build_graph(checkpointer)` that wires the topology AND accepts a pre-built checkpointer via Dependency Injection (no more internal SqliteSaver construction). Public API `start_session` and `resume_session` become `async def` and internally open `AsyncSqliteSaver.from_conn_string(path)` as an async context manager per call — this is the "per-call checkpointer context" pattern from Concept A. Clean lifecycle; tests pass MemorySaver() directly into build_graph. Graph topology (7 nodes + edges + interrupt_after=["3_Weight_Questioner"]) is FROZEN from v1 (AC5).

build_graph_FUNC.NAME: build_graph
build_graph_FUNC.annotation: SYNC factory. Signature: `def build_graph(checkpointer) -> CompiledStateGraph`. Caller is responsible for checkpointer lifecycle. Wires the SAME 7 nodes + conditional edges as v1. Compiles with `checkpointer=checkpointer, interrupt_after=["3_Weight_Questioner"]`.

start_session_FUNC.NAME: start_session
start_session_FUNC.keywords: AsyncPublicAPI; AsyncSqliteSaver; Ainvoke; AgetState; AwaitingUser; AC8; AC9
start_session_FUNC.annotation: `async def start_session(user_input, thread_id, checkpoint_path=None) -> dict`. Opens `async with AsyncSqliteSaver.from_conn_string(path) as cp:`, calls `graph = build_graph(cp)`, `await graph.ainvoke(initial_state, config)` (runs until interrupt after Node 3), reads `snapshot = await graph.aget_state(config)`. Return: {"status":"awaiting_user","question":last_question,"thread_id":thread_id}.

resume_session_FUNC.NAME: resume_session
resume_session_FUNC.keywords: AsyncPublicAPI; AsyncSqliteSaver; AupdateState; Ainvoke; FinalAnswer; AC8
resume_session_FUNC.annotation: `async def resume_session(user_answer, thread_id, checkpoint_path=None) -> dict`. Opens the SAME async checkpointer ctx, rebuilds graph (stateless factory), `await graph.aupdate_state(config, {"user_answer": user_answer})`, `await graph.ainvoke(None, config)` (continues past interrupt through Node 3.5 → 4 → 5 → 6), reads terminal snapshot. Return: {"status":"done","final_answer":final_answer,"thread_id":thread_id}.

stream_session_FUNC.NAME: stream_session
stream_session_FUNC.keywords: AsyncGenerator; AstreamUpdates; PerCallCheckpointerContext; AwaitingUserTerminator; UIStream; AC_UI_03; AC_UI_07
stream_session_FUNC.annotation: NEW in v3.0.0. `async def stream_session(user_input, thread_id, checkpoint_path=None) -> AsyncIterator[dict]`. Opens `async with AsyncSqliteSaver.from_conn_string(path) as cp:`, builds graph via DI, seeds initial_state = {user_input, tool_facts=[], rewrite_count=0}, then `async for chunk in graph.astream(initial_state, config, stream_mode="updates"): yield chunk`. After the stream naturally ends (interrupt reached after 3_Weight_Questioner), reads `snapshot = await graph.aget_state(config)`, yields a final terminator dict `{"__terminator__": "awaiting_user", "question": snapshot.values.get("last_question",""), "thread_id": thread_id}`. Does NOT raise on interrupt — interrupt is the expected terminal state of leg 1. Consumed by src/ui/controllers.py::orchestrate_start. Parallel to start_session (non-streaming variant preserved for smoke_run.py + backend tests).

stream_resume_session_FUNC.NAME: stream_resume_session
stream_resume_session_FUNC.keywords: AsyncGenerator; AstreamUpdates; AupdateState; FinalAnswerTerminator; UIStream; AC_UI_03
stream_resume_session_FUNC.annotation: NEW in v3.0.0. `async def stream_resume_session(user_answer, thread_id, checkpoint_path=None) -> AsyncIterator[dict]`. Opens `async with AsyncSqliteSaver.from_conn_string(path) as cp:`, rebuilds graph, `await graph.aupdate_state(config, {"user_answer": user_answer})`, then `async for chunk in graph.astream(None, config, stream_mode="updates"): yield chunk` (runs through 3.5_Weight_Parser → 4_Draft_Generator → 5_CoVe_Critique (with possible rewrite loop) → 6_Final_Synthesizer → END). After stream ends, reads terminal snapshot and yields `{"__terminator__": "done", "final_answer": snapshot.values.get("final_answer",""), "thread_id": thread_id}`. Consumed by src/ui/controllers.py::orchestrate_resume.

src_features_decision_maker_init_py.annotation: Bumped v1.0.0 → v1.1.0 in v3.0.0. `__all__` extended to re-export streaming variants alongside existing non-streaming API. Current public surface: start_session, resume_session, stream_session, stream_resume_session. No other changes.

src_ui_controllers_py.keywords: UIEvent; AsyncGenerator; FrameworkAgnostic; DomainEventStream; CoVeDetection; NoGradioImport; AC_UI_04; AC_UI_07; AC_UI_08
src_ui_controllers_py.annotation: NEW in v3.0.0. Framework-agnostic async orchestration layer. Does NOT import gradio (AC_UI_04 — verified by test_no_gradio_import_in_controllers_or_presenter). Provides two async-generator orchestration functions that consume stream_session / stream_resume_session and emit a normalised stream of UIEvent dicts for downstream presenter/UI rendering. This is the headless-testable seam: tests mock stream_session with a scripted async generator and assert exact UIEvent counts/ordering. Log classifier: [UIEvent].

UIEvent_TYPEDDICT.NAME: UIEvent
UIEvent_TYPEDDICT.annotation: `class UIEvent(TypedDict, total=False)`. kind: Literal["session_started","node_started","node_completed","state_snapshot","cove_rewrite","awaiting_user","resume_started","final_answer","error"]. Additional optional fields: node, state_delta, state_snapshot, question, critique_feedback, final_answer, error_message, thread_id. The contract between controllers and presenter.

orchestrate_start_FUNC.NAME: orchestrate_start
orchestrate_start_FUNC.annotation: `async def orchestrate_start(user_input, thread_id, checkpoint_path=None) -> AsyncIterator[UIEvent]`. Yields session_started (once, with thread_id), then iterates stream_session. For each astream chunk: emits node_completed (with node name + state_delta) followed by state_snapshot (with aget_state values). On 5_CoVe_Critique chunk where decision="rewrite" and critique_feedback is non-empty, emits cove_rewrite UIEvent BEFORE the corresponding node_completed (ordering contract, AC_UI_08). Final yield: awaiting_user (question from terminator). On exception: error event with error_message.

orchestrate_resume_FUNC.NAME: orchestrate_resume
orchestrate_resume_FUNC.annotation: `async def orchestrate_resume(user_answer, thread_id, checkpoint_path=None) -> AsyncIterator[UIEvent]`. Yields resume_started (once), then iterates stream_resume_session with same node_completed/state_snapshot pair pattern + cove_rewrite injection. Terminates with final_answer UIEvent extracted from terminator.

_extract_cove_rewrite_event_FUNC.NAME: _extract_cove_rewrite_event
_extract_cove_rewrite_event_FUNC.annotation: Pure helper. Input: state_delta dict from a 5_CoVe_Critique chunk. If `state_delta.get("decision") == "rewrite"` AND `state_delta.get("critique_feedback")` truthy → returns UIEvent `{"kind":"cove_rewrite","critique_feedback":...}`, else returns None. Called by both orchestrate_start and orchestrate_resume before emitting node_completed for Node 5.

src_ui_presenter_py.keywords: PureRenderer; UIEventDispatcher; GradioMessagesFormat; StateXRayFilter; RussianStatus; AC_UI_10
src_ui_presenter_py.annotation: NEW in v3.0.0. Pure rendering layer — no I/O, no globals, no async. Converts UIEvent into a tuple of Gradio-renderable deltas: (new_chat_history, new_state_snapshot, status_markdown). Does NOT import gradio itself (chat_history format follows Gradio-5 messages convention as list-of-dicts `{"role":"user"|"assistant","content":str}`). Easily table-tested per AC_UI_10.

render_FUNC.NAME: render
render_FUNC.annotation: `def render(event: UIEvent, chat_history: list[dict], state_snapshot: dict) -> tuple[list[dict], dict, str]`. Dispatches on event["kind"] through a table of handlers (one per 9 UIEvent kinds). session_started/resume_started reset state. node_completed updates status. state_snapshot replaces the right-column JSON (filtered). cove_rewrite appends an assistant message with "⚠️ CoVe-критика:\n\n{critique_feedback}" and sets status "🚨 Критик нашел ошибку, переписываю...". awaiting_user appends assistant turn with the calibration question. final_answer appends assistant turn with final Markdown, sets "✅ Готово". error appends error message and sets "❌" status.

_filter_state_for_display_FUNC.NAME: _filter_state_for_display
_filter_state_for_display_FUNC.annotation: Pure helper. Drops internal/verbose fields (user_input full text, etc.) and keeps the human-readable subset: dilemma, search_queries, tool_facts, weights, rewrite_count, critique_feedback, decision, final_answer. Truncates long string values to 500 chars with ellipsis suffix.

_format_status_FUNC.NAME: _format_status
_format_status_FUNC.annotation: Pure helper. `def _format_status(event_kind: str, node: Optional[str], **extras) -> str`. Maps (kind, node) to Russian emoji-prefixed markdown strings per task spec §2.1: "⏳ Узел 1: Анализ контекста...", "🌐 Выполнение N параллельных запросов...", "🚨 Критик нашел ошибку, переписываю (попытка X/2)", "🤔 Нужен ваш ответ", "✅ Готово", "❌ Ошибка: ...".

src_ui_app_py.keywords: GradioBlocks; BuildUI; OnSubmit; OnNewSession; TwoColumnLayout; ChatbotMessages; JSONStateXRay; UUID4ThreadId; ModeState; LazyImport
src_ui_app_py.annotation: NEW in v3.0.0. SOLE gradio importer in src/ (AC_UI_04). Defines build_ui() -> gr.Blocks factory for scripts/run_brainstorm_ui.py. Two-column layout per task spec §2.2: LEFT = gr.Chatbot(type="messages"), gr.Textbox, gr.Button("Отправить"), gr.Markdown for status, "Новая сессия" button; RIGHT = gr.JSON(label="State X-Ray"). gr.State holds thread_id (uuid4), mode ("awaiting_submit" | "awaiting_user_answer"), chat_history list, state_snapshot dict. Log classifier: [UIEvent].

build_ui_FUNC.NAME: build_ui
build_ui_FUNC.annotation: `def build_ui() -> gr.Blocks`. Composes the two-column Blocks, instantiates all Gradio components, binds on_submit to the submit button's click handler (and textbox submit), binds on_new_session to the reset button.

on_submit_FUNC.NAME: on_submit
on_submit_FUNC.keywords: AsyncGeneratorHandler; ModeDispatch; RenderLoop; SixSlotTuple; AC_UI_09; AC_UI_17
on_submit_FUNC.annotation: `async def on_submit(user_input, thread_id, mode, chat_history, state_snapshot) -> AsyncIterator[tuple]`. Dispatches: `mode=="awaiting_submit"` → orchestrate_start; `mode=="awaiting_user_answer"` → orchestrate_resume. For each UIEvent: calls render(), yields 6-slot tuple `(chat_history, state_snapshot, status_md, new_mode, thread_id, "")`. Mode transitions at awaiting_user (→ "awaiting_user_answer") and final_answer (→ "awaiting_submit"). Thread_id persists across events (AC_UI_17). Emits [BeliefState][IMP:9] logs at dispatch and completion.

on_new_session_FUNC.NAME: on_new_session
on_new_session_FUNC.annotation: `def on_new_session() -> tuple`. Generates fresh uuid4 thread_id, clears chat_history to [], resets state_snapshot to {}, sets status to "Готов принять дилемму.", mode to "awaiting_submit". Bound to "Новая сессия" button.

src_ui_init_py.annotation: NEW in v3.0.0. Re-exports `build_ui` from app.py for scripts/run_brainstorm_ui.py consumption. Internal controllers/presenter modules are implementation detail and NOT re-exported.

tests_conftest_py.keywords: Conftest; AsyncFixtures; MemorySaver; FakeLlm; FakeSearchAsync; AntiLoopSessionHook; EventLoop; LDDCapture
tests_conftest_py.annotation: REFACTORED in v2.0.0. Preserves Anti-Loop session hooks (.test_counter.json) verbatim. NEW fixtures: (a) event_loop_policy (pytest-asyncio session scope), (b) memory_checkpointer yielding fresh MemorySaver() per test, (c) fake_llm with async-aware .ainvoke(messages)→AIMessage, (d) fake_search_async with configurable sleep delay (DELAY=0.2s) used by test_parallel_search to prove concurrency. ldd_capture(caplog) helper preserved.

tests_test_routing_py.annotation: SYNC — routers are sync. Asserts double-True raises DoubleTrueError; {needs_data:true} → "tool"; {ready_for_weights:true} → "questioner".

tests_test_anti_loop_py.keywords: AsyncTest; PytestAsyncio; AntiLoop; IMP10; BLOCK_ANTI_LOOP; AC6
tests_test_anti_loop_py.annotation: @pytest.mark.asyncio. Seeds state with rewrite_count=2, awaits cove_critique(state, llm_factory=fake_llm_factory), asserts returned dict has rewrite_count unchanged AND [IMP:10][BLOCK_ANTI_LOOP][SafetyTripped] log line present. Then calls SYNC route_from_critique → asserts "finalize".

tests_test_graph_compilation_py.keywords: SyncTest; GraphCompile; MemorySaver; BaseCheckpointSaver; InterruptAfter; AC5; AC10
tests_test_graph_compilation_py.annotation: Compiles graph using memory_checkpointer fixture (MemorySaver — no SQLite DB file). Asserts all 7 node IDs reachable from START and that interrupt_after includes "3_Weight_Questioner". Checkpointer isinstance check relaxed to BaseCheckpointSaver so the same test covers both MemorySaver and AsyncSqliteSaver paths.

tests_test_json_utils_py.keywords: SyncTest; JsonParser; JsonParseError; RawSnippet; ReasoningPrefixCases; ParametrizedPositive; SnippetPreservation
tests_test_json_utils_py.annotation: EXTENDED in v1.1.0 (6 → 12 tests). Original v1.0.0 cases preserved: raw json, fenced ```json, bare ```, embedded prose, empty input → JsonParseError, malformed → JsonParseError.raw_snippet populated. NEW in v1.1.0 (regression coverage for Strategy 0 + raw_decode fallback): 5 parametrized positive cases — thinking_prefix_simple, thinking_prefix_uppercase, thinking_with_nested_braces, think_short_tag, reasoning_block_before_fenced_json; plus 1 standalone test_thinking_block_stripped_preserves_original_snippet_on_error asserting JsonParseError.raw_snippet reflects the ORIGINAL input (with <thinking> prefix intact) rather than the normalised text. All 12 pass under pytest-asyncio-free sync execution.

tests_test_async_core_py.keywords: AsyncTest; PytestAsyncio; AsyncSqliteSaver; EventLoopBoundary; CheckpointRoundTrip; AC8; AC9; AC10
tests_test_async_core_py.annotation: NEW in v2.0.0. Two @pytest.mark.asyncio tests: (1) test_async_start_session_reaches_interrupt — awaits start_session against AsyncSqliteSaver at tmp_path DB, asserts status="awaiting_user" and question non-empty; uses fake_llm for nodes 1..3. (2) test_async_resume_session_preserves_state — chains start+resume on SAME tmp_path DB with SAME thread_id, asserts final_answer non-empty AND terminal state contains weights/draft_analysis/final_answer (proves AsyncSqliteSaver checkpoint round-trip survives event-loop transitions).

tests_test_llm_utils_py.keywords: UnitTest; PytestAsyncio; LLMSanitization; Idempotent; LastOutputBlockPreference; IntegrationRegression; WeightQuestionerFix; FinalSynthesizerFix; CleanedLengthLogAssertion
tests_test_llm_utils_py.annotation: NEW in v2.1.0 (Option D). 14 tests total across four groups: (1) strip_reasoning_blocks unit (5): basic, uppercase, multi-tag, nested braces inside thinking, no-tags-unchanged. (2) extract_output_payload unit (5): present, absent-returns-unchanged, multi-line Markdown payload, case-insensitive, MULTIPLE blocks returns-LAST. (3) sanitize_llm_response unit (3): full pipeline grok-style, idempotent sanitize(sanitize(x)) == sanitize(x), empty/whitespace-only input. (4) integration regressions (3, pytest-asyncio): - test_weight_questioner_extracts_last_question_from_grok_style_response — proves the fix for bug #1 (last_question='' became 'Какие критерии важнее?'). - test_final_synthesizer_strips_reasoning_from_final_answer — proves the fix for bug #2 (final_answer contains Markdown header, 0 occurrences of <thinking> or <output>). - test_invoke_llm_async_emits_cleaned_length_log — proves the new BLOCK_SANITIZE LDD log line is emitted with both raw_length and cleaned_length markers.

tests_test_parallel_search_py.keywords: AsyncTest; PytestAsyncio; AsyncioGather; WallClockProof; PerQueryLDD; AC11; AC13
tests_test_parallel_search_py.annotation: NEW in v2.0.0. @pytest.mark.asyncio tests proving concurrency. Constructs 3 queries; injects fake_search_async (DELAY=0.2s). Awaits tool_node(state, search_fn=fake_search_async); asserts wall-clock elapsed < DELAY × N × 0.75 = 0.45s (sequential would be ≥0.6s). Second test asserts per-query IMP:7 PENDING and IMP:8 SUCCESS both present in caplog (exactly one pair per query — AC13 LDD parallel integrity).

requirements_txt.annotation: v2.0.0 pins. Preserves all v1 pins (langgraph==0.2.60, langgraph-checkpoint-sqlite==2.0.1, langchain-core==0.3.21, langchain-openai==0.2.10, pydantic==2.9.2, python-dotenv==1.0.1, pytest==8.3.3, langsmith==0.1.147). Adds async dependencies: aiosqlite==0.20.0 (AsyncSqliteSaver driver), tavily-python==0.5.0 (AsyncTavilyClient), pytest-asyncio==0.24.0 (@pytest.mark.asyncio runtime). Each new pin carries a WHY-comment per core-rules §3.

env_example.annotation: v2.0.0. Contains OPENROUTER_API_KEY, OPENROUTER_MODEL (default: x-ai/grok-4-fast), TAVILY_API_KEY placeholder, and commented-out LangSmith keys. TAVILY_API_KEY is consumed by tools.search_async at call-time; absence triggers stub_search_async fallback.

scripts_smoke_run_py.keywords: SmokeTest; EndToEnd; RealNetwork; OpenRouter; Tavily; AsyncIO; DotEnvLoader; TempCheckpoint; LDDTailDump; OperatorTool
scripts_smoke_run_py.annotation: NEW in v2.0.1 (created during post-QA real-network verification cycle). One-shot CLI runner for end-to-end smoke testing against REAL external services (OpenRouter LLM + Tavily search). Loads brainstorm/.env via python-dotenv BEFORE importing src.features.*; refuses to run on placeholder keys (ends-with "..." heuristic). Uses tempfile.mkdtemp() for an isolated per-run checkpoint DB — never pollutes production brainstorm/checkpoints.sqlite. Exercises await start_session → canned human weights reply → await resume_session, prints the returned dicts, and dumps the tail IMP:7-10 lines from decision_maker.log for operator inspection. NOT part of the pytest suite.

run_smoke_FUNC.NAME: run_smoke
run_smoke_FUNC.annotation: async def run_smoke() -> None. Builds a realistic Russian-language financial dilemma, threads a fresh UUID-based thread_id, awaits start_session, feeds a canned weights answer (Безопасность 40% / гибкость 30% / доходность 20% / комфорт 10%) into resume_session, prints results.

dump_ldd_tail_FUNC.NAME: dump_ldd_tail
dump_ldd_tail_FUNC.annotation: def dump_ldd_tail(log_path: Path, tail_lines: int = 400) -> None. Reads the last N lines of decision_maker.log and prints only those containing [IMP:7] / [IMP:8] / [IMP:9] / [IMP:10] markers — surfaces per-query parallel evidence + BeliefState writes + Anti-Loop/ExceptionEnrichment lines for visual verification after a smoke run.

scripts_run_brainstorm_ui_py.keywords: GradioLauncher; OperatorTool; LazyImport; DotEnvLoader; EnvVarSanityCheck; InBrowser; LocalHostOnly; KeyboardInterruptGuard
scripts_run_brainstorm_ui_py.annotation: NEW in v3.0.0. Operator entry-point for the Gradio UI. Mirrors scripts/smoke_run.py topology exactly: prepends project root to sys.path, loads .env BEFORE importing feature modules, validates required env vars (OPENROUTER_API_KEY, OPENROUTER_MODEL, TAVILY_API_KEY) with placeholder detection, lazy-imports gradio + src.ui.app.build_ui (so environment failures surface before heavy UI instantiation), invokes demo = build_ui(), demo.launch(server_name="127.0.0.1", inbrowser=True), wrapped in try/except KeyboardInterrupt for clean Ctrl-C shutdown. NOT part of the pytest suite.

main_FUNC.NAME: main
main_FUNC.annotation: `def main() -> None`. Executes the env-validation + lazy-import + launch sequence described above.

tests_ui_conftest_py.keywords: AntiLoopProtocol; SessionHooks; TestCounter; PytestAsyncioAuto; LddCapture; AC_UI_13
tests_ui_conftest_py.annotation: NEW in v3.0.0. Anti-Loop Protocol conftest per mode-code §Step 4: pytest_sessionstart / pytest_sessionfinish manage tests/ui/.test_counter.json (increments on failures, resets to 0 only on 100% pass). Configures pytest-asyncio mode="auto". Provides ldd_capture fixture that filters caplog records by `[IMP:7-10]` regex and prints them to stdout BEFORE asserts (Anti-Illusion — agent sees trajectory on failure). Checklist + reflection output activated on attempt >0.

tests_ui_test_presenter_py.annotation: NEW in v3.0.0. 9 table-driven tests — one per UIEvent kind. Each test asserts render(event, chat_history, state_snapshot) returns tuple of shape (list, dict, str) with expected delta semantics: session_started resets state, node_completed appends status, state_snapshot updates JSON, cove_rewrite appends assistant warning + sets red status, awaiting_user appends question, final_answer appends final markdown + sets green status, error sets red status + appends error message.

tests_ui_test_state_streaming_py.keywords: StreamFidelity; MockedAstream; ThreeUpdates; CoVeOrdering; AC_UI_07; AC_UI_08
tests_ui_test_state_streaming_py.annotation: NEW in v3.0.0. 4 tests. Core: test_three_updates_produce_three_uievents — monkeypatches stream_session with a fake async generator yielding 3 state-update chunks + awaiting_user terminator; collects `events = [ev async for ev in orchestrate_start("x","tid")]`; asserts `len([e for e in events if e["kind"]=="node_completed"]) == 3` AND `len(state_snapshot) == 3` AND final `awaiting_user`. Core: test_cove_rewrite_emits_dedicated_event — fake chunk for 5_CoVe_Critique with decision="rewrite" + critique_feedback → asserts cove_rewrite UIEvent is emitted BEFORE the corresponding node_completed in the stream ordering.

tests_ui_test_ui_handlers_py.keywords: HeadlessUI; HandlerGenerator; SixSlotTuple; ThreadIdContinuity; NoGradioLaunch; AC_UI_09; AC_UI_11; AC_UI_17
tests_ui_test_ui_handlers_py.annotation: NEW in v3.0.0. 6 tests. Verifies on_submit is an async generator, yields 6-slot tuples matching the contract (chat, state_json, status, new_mode, thread_id, textbox_clear). test_on_submit_transitions_to_awaiting_user_answer — after awaiting_user UIEvent, final tuple contains new_mode="awaiting_user_answer" (AC_UI_09). test_on_submit_thread_id_continuity — thread_id passed in equals thread_id in final tuple (AC_UI_17). test_no_gradio_import_in_controllers_or_presenter — grep-style import check (AC_UI_11 enforcement). NEVER calls `.launch()`.

decision_maker_log.keywords: LDD; RuntimeLog; IMP7; IMP8; IMP9; IMP10; BlockAntiLoop; BlockExecuteSearches; PerQueryTelemetry
decision_maker_log.annotation: Runtime LDD log file. Produced by setup_ldd_logger FileHandler. After test run, contains IMP:9 BeliefState lines from cove_critique AND multiple IMP:7/IMP:8 pairs from tool_node with distinct query= values (AC20). IMP:10 BLOCK_ANTI_LOOP lines appear when Anti-Loop cap trips in test_anti_loop.

src_server_config_py.keywords: pydantic-settings; SecretStr; EnvDriven; FailFast; SweepThresholdValidator; MCP_Integration
src_server_config_py.annotation: Pydantic-settings configuration for the brainstorm MCP HTTP server. Single source of truth for all runtime parameters. Required fields (BRAINSTORM_HMAC_SECRET, GATEWAY_LLM_PROXY_URL, GATEWAY_LLM_API_KEY) raise ValidationError at startup on absence — fail-fast before any socket opens. SecretStr prevents accidental secret logging. model_validator enforces sweep_threshold_secs >= 5 * turn_timeout_sec (§9.4 race safety). All downstream modules receive a typed Config instance via get_cfg() Depends provider — never raw os.environ access.

Config_CLASS.NAME: Config
Config_CLASS.annotation: Frozen configuration snapshot. Fields: hmac_secret (BRAINSTORM_HMAC_SECRET), gateway_llm_api_key (GATEWAY_LLM_API_KEY), gateway_llm_proxy_url (GATEWAY_LLM_PROXY_URL), checkpointer_kind (sqlite|postgres), sqlite_path, checkpoint_dsn, session_ttl_sec, turn_timeout_sec, sweep_interval_sec, sweep_threshold_secs, llm_model, gradio_ui, log_level, metrics_port.

get_cfg_FUNC.NAME: get_cfg
get_cfg_FUNC.annotation: FastAPI Depends-compatible provider. lru_cache(maxsize=1) ensures env parsing runs exactly once per process. Logs IMP:9 belief-state on first call. Tests use dependency_overrides or get_cfg.cache_clear().

src_server_auth_py.keywords: HMAC_SHA256; base64url; ConstantTimeCompare; ZeroKnowledgeClaims; PadRepair; GoWireCompat; TokenMint; R4_ZeroKnowledge; I5_ThreeFieldShape; I6_PopPII; I7_ConstantTime; I8_PadRepair
src_server_auth_py.annotation: Python port of crablink-gateway/kernel/sessiontoken/token.go v1.0.0 verifier with R4 zero-knowledge payload interpretation. Provides verify_session_token (full HMAC pipeline), mint_session_token (v1.1.0 addition — inverse for dev tooling), require_service FastAPI Depends factory, and _b64url_encode/_b64url_decode base64url primitives. I5: TokenClaims has exactly {service_id, session_id, exp}. I6: user_id and iat are popped immediately after json.loads. I7: hmac.compare_digest for constant-time comparison. I8: pad-repair for Go RawURLEncoding compatibility.

TokenClaims_CLASS.NAME: TokenClaims
TokenClaims_CLASS.annotation: @dataclass(frozen=True) with exactly {service_id, session_id, exp}. Never contains user_id or iat — R4 zero-knowledge structural invariant enforced by test_token_claims_strict_shape.

AuthError_CLASS.NAME: AuthError
AuthError_CLASS.annotation: Exception carrying .reason in {malformed, bad_version, bad_signature, expired, wrong_service, missing_session}. Used by Prometheus metric label brainstorm_token_verify_failures_total{reason=...}.

verify_session_token_FUNC.NAME: verify_session_token
verify_session_token_FUNC.keywords: BearerParse; SplitDot; Base64urlDecode; HMAC_Verify; JSONParse; PopPII; ServiceValidate; UUIDv4Check; ExpiryCheck; IMP9_BeliefState
verify_session_token_FUNC.annotation: 12-step pipeline: Bearer-strip → split(.) → version v1 → base64url-decode(pad-repair) → HMAC-SHA256 compare_digest → json.loads → pop user_id/iat → assert absent → construct TokenClaims → validate service_id/session_id/exp. Logs IMP:9 on both failure and success (AI Belief State).

mint_session_token_FUNC.NAME: mint_session_token
mint_session_token_FUNC.keywords: DevHelper; TokenMint; GoWireCompat; InverseOfVerify; SortedKeys; CompactJSON
mint_session_token_FUNC.annotation: v1.1.0 addition (Slice E). Inverse of verify_session_token: builds payload JSON (sorted keys, compact), computes HMAC-SHA256, base64url-encodes both, returns "v1.{payload}.{sig}". Used ONLY by scripts/mint_token.py and tests/smoke/test_mint_roundtrip.py. NOT for production request handling.

require_service_FUNC.NAME: require_service
require_service_FUNC.annotation: Factory returning stable closures from _DEPS_CACHE. Uses setdefault to guarantee identity equality for FastAPI dependency_overrides in tests. Inner closure reads Authorization header via Header(None, alias="Authorization"), calls verify_session_token, translates AuthError to HTTPException(401|403).

src_server_checkpoint_factory_py.keywords: LangGraph; SqliteSaver; PostgresSaver; FactoryPattern; WAL; setup; ConfigError; TouchMetadata
src_server_checkpoint_factory_py.annotation: Pure factory returning a BaseCheckpointSaver. build_checkpointer switches on cfg.checkpointer_kind: sqlite (AsyncSqliteSaver, WAL-enabled via PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL at setup) or postgres (EXPERIMENTAL). Unknown kind raises ConfigError. lifespan calls saver.setup() for schema migration. Also provides get_checkpointer FastAPI Depends returning app.state.checkpointer. Touch-based metadata (_brainstorm_meta table) records last_touched per thread_id for sweeper race safety (§9.4).

build_checkpointer_FUNC.NAME: build_checkpointer
build_checkpointer_FUNC.keywords: SqliteSaver; PostgresSaver; WAL; setup; ConfigError; FactorySwitch
build_checkpointer_FUNC.annotation: Switches on cfg.checkpointer_kind. Calls saver.setup() once for schema migration. Returns configured BaseCheckpointSaver instance.

get_checkpointer_FUNC.NAME: get_checkpointer
get_checkpointer_FUNC.annotation: Returns app.state.checkpointer. Overridable in tests via dependency_overrides.

src_server_sweeper_py.keywords: asyncio; TTL; IdleSweep; TouchBased; SWEEP_THRESHOLD_SECS; IMP5_Cleanup; active_sessions
src_server_sweeper_py.annotation: Background asyncio coroutine that periodically scans the checkpointer for stale sessions and deletes them. Uses touch-based predicate: last_touched < now - SWEEP_THRESHOLD_SECS (not last_update). Emits IMP:5 [Sweep][Cleanup] per deleted session and IMP:5 [Sweep][Summary] per scan cycle. Updates brainstorm_active_sessions gauge on deletion. Single-writer safe with the WAL checkpointer (LockRegistry deferred to BACKLOG).

Sweeper_CLASS.NAME: Sweeper
Sweeper_CLASS.annotation: Constructor receives checkpointer, session_ttl_sec, sweep_interval_sec. run() is the long-running asyncio coroutine: sleep → scan → delete stale → log → repeat.

Sweeper_run_METHOD.NAME: run
Sweeper_run_METHOD.annotation: Infinite loop: await asyncio.sleep(interval) → list stale sessions → delete each → update active_sessions gauge → log IMP:5 Summary.

src_server_metrics_py.keywords: prometheus_client; Counter; Histogram; Gauge; CollectorRegistry; brainstorm_prefix; 10MetricFamilies
src_server_metrics_py.annotation: Single-registry Prometheus metrics namespace. build_registry() creates a CollectorRegistry with all 10 brainstorm_* metric families: brainstorm_turns_total (Counter), brainstorm_turn_duration_seconds (Histogram), brainstorm_llm_roundtrip_seconds (Histogram), brainstorm_active_sessions (Gauge), brainstorm_done_total (Counter), brainstorm_token_verify_failures_total (Counter, label reason), brainstorm_idempotent_hits_total (Counter, label source), brainstorm_sweeper_runs_total (Counter), brainstorm_sweeper_deleted_total (Counter), brainstorm_readyz_checks_total (Counter). Exposed via /metrics endpoint on main port.

build_registry_FUNC.NAME: build_registry
build_registry_FUNC.annotation: Builds a CollectorRegistry used by the FastAPI app and scraped at /metrics. Also used in tests for metric-delta assertions.

src_server_errors_py.annotation: Domain exception types and their translation to HTTP responses. ConfigError for unsupported checkpointer kind. LLMTimeoutError for asyncio.TimeoutError in /turn handler. AuthError is defined in auth.py but its HTTPException translation lives here or in the require_service closure. All error responses include X-Correlation-ID header via CorrelationIDMiddleware.

src_server_idempotency_py.keywords: TTLCache; cachetools; IdempotencyKey; asyncio_Lock; HeaderKey; InternalTupleKey; 600sTTL; PerPodCache
src_server_idempotency_py.annotation: IdempotencyCache wrapping cachetools.TTLCache(maxsize=10000, ttl=600). Protected by asyncio.Lock for coroutine-safe lookup+insert. Key source: Idempotency-Key header (validated against ^[a-zA-Z0-9_\-]{8,128}$) if present; otherwise sha256(session_id + NUL + message + NUL + turn_n)[:32]. Cache is per-pod (Redis deferred to BACKLOG for multi-replica). Metric brainstorm_idempotent_hits_total{source="header"|"internal"} incremented on cache hit.

IdempotencyCache_CLASS.NAME: IdempotencyCache
IdempotencyCache_CLASS.annotation: get(key) and set(key, value) are async, guarded by Lock. Malformed Idempotency-Key header → fall back to internal tuple key, log IMP:5 MalformedKey.

src_server_middleware_py.keywords: CorrelationID; X-Correlation-ID; UUID4Fallback; RequestState; ResponseHeader; MalformedHeaderFingerprint; IMP5_Trace
src_server_middleware_py.annotation: Starlette middleware that reads X-Correlation-ID request header, validates against ^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]{8,64}$: match → use as-is; malformed → uuid4 + log IMP:5 MalformedCorrelationID with fingerprint; absent → uuid4 silently. Attaches to request.state.correlation_id. Copies to every response as X-Correlation-ID (even error responses).

src_server_turn_api_py.keywords: FastAPI; APIRouter; POST_turn; POST_done; GET_healthz; GET_readyz; GET_metrics; Lifespan; TurnResponse; DoneResponse; IMP7_TurnBoundary; IMP4_RequestIn
src_server_turn_api_py.annotation: APIRouter with all 5 endpoints. /turn and /done require Depends(require_service("brainstorm")). /healthz, /readyz, /metrics are public. handle_turn: auth → body parse → idempotency check → session routing → stream_session/stream_resume_session → metrics → LDD IMP:7 Turn Start/End. handle_done: auth → delete checkpoint idempotently → 200. handle_readyz: checkpointer ping + LLM gateway HTTP probe (httpx, 2s timeout) → 200 or 503. handle_healthz: 200 unconditional.

handle_turn_FUNC.NAME: handle_turn
handle_turn_FUNC.keywords: TurnHappyPath; CheckpointLoad; StreamSession; asyncio_wait_for; LLMTimeout; IMP7_Turn; IMP9_BeliefState
handle_turn_FUNC.annotation: Main turn handler. Reads Idempotency-Key header. Routes to start or resume session. Wraps LLM call in asyncio.wait_for(timeout=cfg.turn_timeout_sec). Returns TurnResponse(session_id, reply, state, metadata).

handle_done_FUNC.NAME: handle_done
handle_done_FUNC.annotation: Deletes checkpoint for session_id. Swallows NotFound — returns {"acknowledged": true} unconditionally. Decrements active_sessions gauge only if deletion found an existing session.

handle_healthz_FUNC.NAME: handle_healthz
handle_healthz_FUNC.annotation: Public liveness probe. Returns {"status":"ok"} unconditionally. No auth, no IO, no metric emission.

handle_readyz_FUNC.NAME: handle_readyz
handle_readyz_FUNC.annotation: Public readiness probe. Pings checkpointer (SELECT 1) and LLM gateway (httpx GET /healthz, 2s timeout). Returns 200 {status:ready} or 503 {status:not_ready, diag}.

src_server_app_factory_py.keywords: FastAPI; Lifespan; create_app; DependencyOverrides; CorrelationIDMiddleware; MetricsMount; Sweeper; UvicornFactory
src_server_app_factory_py.annotation: create_app(cfg) factory: builds FastAPI instance with lifespan context manager that (startup) calls build_checkpointer, checkpointer.setup(), stores on app.state, builds llm_client, starts Sweeper asyncio task; (shutdown) cancels sweeper, calls checkpointer.close(). Mounts CorrelationIDMiddleware. Registers APIRouter from turn_api.py. Mounts /metrics on main port. Used as --factory entry point by uvicorn Dockerfile CMD.

create_app_FUNC.NAME: create_app
create_app_FUNC.keywords: FactoryPattern; Lifespan; AppState; SweeperTask; MetricsMounting; UvicornFactory
create_app_FUNC.annotation: Injectable for tests (TestClient(create_app(test_cfg))). Lifespan startup/shutdown LDD at IMP:7.

scripts_mint_token_py.keywords: DevOnly; TokenMint; CLI; HMAC_SHA256; GoWireCompat; DelegateToCanonical; argparse
scripts_mint_token_py.annotation: Developer-only CLI helper. Mints v1 session tokens by delegating to src.server.auth.mint_session_token — does NOT reimplement HMAC. Arguments: --service-id (default brainstorm), --session-id (default uuid4), --ttl (required, seconds), --secret-env (default BRAINSTORM_HMAC_SECRET). Prints "Bearer v1.<payload>.<sig>" to stdout. Warnings to stderr. Secret fingerprint sha256:<8hex> only. 16-byte minimum secret check.

scripts_smoke_brainstorm_py.keywords: L2Smoke; CISkippable; BRAINSTORM_SMOKE; 7Checks; StructuredBanner; httpx; MetricDelta; IdempotencyKeyCheck
scripts_smoke_brainstorm_py.annotation: L2 smoke test script. CI-skippable: exits 0 if BRAINSTORM_SMOKE != "1". 7 checks: (1) healthz 200 <=2s, (2) readyz 200 checkpointer+llm, (3) metrics 200 text/plain + 10 families, (4) /turn no-auth 401 + X-Correlation-ID header, (5) forged token 401 + metric delta, (6) valid turn 200 reply + Idempotency-Key hit, (7) /done twice 200 idempotent. Uses httpx sync client. Inline _mint_bearer_inline (no src.server import). Prints [SMOKE][N/7][name] PASS/FAIL (rtt=Xms) banners. Exit 0 = all PASS, exit 1 = any FAIL.

tests_smoke_test_mint_roundtrip_py.keywords: UnitTest; MintVerifyRoundtrip; TamperTest; ExpiryTest; WrongService; WrongSecret; I5_Invariant; AntiLoop; LDD_Telemetry; pytest_mark_unit
tests_smoke_test_mint_roundtrip_py.annotation: Pure unit tests for mint_session_token -> verify_session_token roundtrip. 7 tests: I5 shape invariant, happy-path roundtrip with IMP:9 log assertion, tampered-sig bad_signature, tampered-payload bad_signature, expired token, wrong_service, wrong_secret. No network. Anti-Loop conftest in tests/smoke/conftest.py. All @pytest.mark.unit.

k8s_deployment_section.keywords: Kubernetes; kustomize; StatefulSet; Replicas1; Workers1; Hardlock; PVC; HMAC_Secret; ConfigMap; Service; Manifest
k8s_deployment_section.annotation: Kubernetes deployment artifacts in k8s/. StatefulSet with replicas=1 hardlocked (sqlite MVP — no multi-replica without Postgres). PVC at /data for SQLite checkpoint. Secrets injected via k8s Secret. Non-root uid 10001, readOnlyRootFilesystem, capabilities drop ALL. CMD: uvicorn src.server.app_factory:create_app --factory --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 --workers 1. Manifest file brainstorm.manifest.json declares MCP tools brainstorm__turn / brainstorm__done for gateway operator registration.

k8s_deployment_yaml.annotation: StatefulSet: replicas=1 hardlocked. BRAINSTORM_HMAC_SECRET and GATEWAY_LLM_API_KEY from Secret. GATEWAY_LLM_PROXY_URL and other config from ConfigMap. Liveness probe /healthz. Readiness probe /readyz. volumeMount /data for PVC.

k8s_brainstorm_manifest_json.annotation: MCP manifest for gateway operator registration. Declares brainstorm__turn (POST /turn) and brainstorm__done (POST /done) tools with JSON schemas. Never auto-registered by the brainstorm service itself — constraint #12.

Dockerfile.annotation: Multi-stage python:3.12-slim. Non-root USER 10001. CMD uvicorn factory --workers 1. HEALTHCHECK /healthz. .dockerignore excludes .venv, __pycache__, *.sqlite, *.log, *.pyc.

docs_section.keywords: README; SECURITY; BACKLOG; MCP_API; Configuration; Deployment; ManifestRegistration; ThreatModel; HMAC_WireFormat; RaceAnalysis; DeferredItems
docs_section.annotation: Slice E documentation layer. README.md extended with MCP HTTP API, Configuration table (14 env vars), Deployment (docker + kubectl), Manifest registration (curl POST), Security model (link to SECURITY.md). SECURITY.md: threat model (untrusted callers, replay, forgery), HMAC wire format, constant-time compare, logging policy, race analysis (sweeper 600s safe window), operational secrets (BRAINSTORM_HMAC_SECRET, GATEWAY_LLM_API_KEY, rotation procedure), container hardening (uid 10001, readOnlyRootFilesystem, caps drop ALL, replicas=1). BACKLOG.md: 7 deferred items (Postgres readiness, R4 re-mint, HMAC rotation, LockRegistry, hadolint, kustomize migration, Redis idempotency cache).

README_md.annotation: Extended in v4.0.0 with: MCP HTTP API (5 endpoints), Configuration table, Deployment, Manifest registration, Security model sections appended after existing content.

SECURITY_md.annotation: NEW in Slice E. Threat model, HMAC wire format, constant-time compare rationale, logging policy, race analysis, operational secrets, container hardening, zero-knowledge domain invariant.

BACKLOG_md.annotation: NEW in Slice E. 7 deferred items with one-line rationale. Replaces TODO/FIXME in production code (constraint #9).
